title: cerastium caespitosum subsp. glandulosum
intro: Cerastium caespitosum subsp. glandulosum, also known as a glandular downy chickweed, is a subspecies of the Cerastium caespitosum plant, belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It is a perennial herb native to western North America, known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, and may be used to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Traditionally, herbal preparations of Cerastium caespitosum subsp. glandulosum include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional.
